author: Austin Crawford
title: Magic Shows with Rob Martin
summary: Rob Martin is back this year with a whole new show! Rob Martin's Magic Show is now family friendly. 21+ can still bring your own wine. The show schedule and pricing have changed but it still has limited seating, so don't delay. Make sure you are signed up while you are here on vacation. You won't see this show anywhere else and it is the only magic show on Block Island!
content:
Rob Martin is back this year with a whole new show! Rob Martin's Magic Show is now family friendly. 21+ can still bring your own wine. The show schedule and pricing have changed but it still has limited seating, so don't delay. Make sure you are signed up while you are here on vacation. You won't see this show anywhere else and it is the only magic show on Block Island!

 

Excite your night, with Rob Martin's Magic Show, a visual and mental close-up magic show that gives you the rare opportunity to witness expert close-up magic. Join us, while we take a magical journey into the wild imagination of magician, Rob Martin. This isn’t your average kid’s magic show, but it is family friendly. To keep the show intimate and interactive, there will be no more than 25 guests per show. Please book online or call us to reserve your seat.

 

Rob Martin's Magic Show can be booked for private groups in house. Rob is also available for on location magic for events and parties. Please contact us via email or phone to inquire and check availability for private events.

 

Location: Block Island Ghost Tours on the second floor of the Odd Fellows Hall

Time: 8pm on Wednesdays, 6pm on Fridays, 4pm & 8pm on Saturdays

Length: 45 mins

Price: $40/person, $30 Children 12 & under

blockislandghosttours@gmail.com, 

401-477-2979

 

Click the link below for tickets.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Learn How to Shuck Oysters
summary: Shucking class at Old Island Pub, this Saturday, 4 to 5pm.
content:
Shucking class at Old Island Pub, this Saturday, 4 to 5pm.

Have you been wanting to learn how to shuck an oyster?????

Many people do, so we are offering a shucking class and you will get a hands- on lesson, eight oysters to shuck and eat, a hinge shucking knife, all for $35.00.  This will be our last of the season (we think).  

OIP beverages will be available to complement your learning and tasting experience.  We are limiting the class to 12 people so we can provide individual attention.

Please email us at sales@sunfarmoystersbi.com if you have any questions.

To sign up click the link below:

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Gardeners Fall Wrap-up
summary: All are invited to the Block Island Gardeners Fall Wrap-up at the Community Center. Enjoy light refreshments and participate in a discussion of this year’s gardening experiences (successes, failures and opportunities for learning). Followed at 5 p.m. by a talk, “Gardening Above and Below the Ocean: Kelp Harvesting on Block Island,” presented by Catherine Puckett. For questions, email Elizabeth at moosster@gmail.com.
content: All are invited to the Block Island Gardeners Fall Wrap-up at the Community Center. Enjoy light refreshments and participate in a discussion of this year’s gardening experiences (successes, failures and opportunities for learning). Followed at 5 p.m. by a talk, “Gardening Above and Below the Ocean: Kelp Harvesting on Block Island,” presented by Catherine Puckett. For questions, email Elizabeth at moosster@gmail.com.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Arnold Weinstein discusses his new book “The Lives of Literature”
summary: Join us for an afternoon of reading and conversation with Brown professor and author Arnold Weinstein as he reads from and discusses his most recent work, The Lives of Literature. A work that is both personal reflection and commentary on the broader impact books have on our lives, The Lives of Literature asks the question: why do we read literature? Light refreshments will be served, and all are welcome.
content: Join us for an afternoon of reading and conversation with Brown professor and author Arnold Weinstein as he reads from and discusses his most recent work, The Lives of Literature. A work that is both personal reflection and commentary on the broader impact books have on our lives, The Lives of Literature asks the question: why do we read literature? Light refreshments will be served, and all are welcome.

summary: Guest speaker will be Kate Venturini-Hardesty, Program Administrator for the U.R.I. Master Gardener Program and head of its Leadership Board of Directors. Any questions, please call Maude at (203) 233-4889.

author: Robert Lucier
title: Electric and Antique Car Show
summary: Come and see the brand new Ford MACH-E, also known as the electric Mustang! Talk to a sales rep and maybe buy it on the spot! A big Thank You to Flood Ford.
content:
Come and see the brand new Ford MACH-E, also known as the electric Mustang! Talk to a sales rep and maybe buy it on the spot! A big Thank You to Flood Ford.


*See EVs that call Block Island home and discuss their performance with their owners.
*See a sampling of the many antiques that live on BI--cars i mean!
*See a brand new Tesla---you've never seen a car like this!
*Test ride an electric bike and more!
address: Harbor Baptist Church 21 Water St New Shoreham

author: Robert Lucier
title: Film Screening & Book Signing
summary: Film screening and book signing with Edith Littlefield Blane for her book Bound by Blue at the Block Island Historical Society.
content:
Film screening and book signing with Edith Littlefield Blane for her book Bound by Blue at the Block Island Historical Society.

The book signing is 5 pm to 6 pm.  Limited seating - lemonade and a few island stories. Public welcome.

Signed books available at Museum Shop in advance.  Open 10 am to 5 pm.

content:
The Nature Conservancy is hosting an Open House to give thanks to the Island community for supporting our program. Please stop by after the stroll for some holiday cheer!

 

Provided By: Block Island Tourism Council

author: Robert Lucier
title: BIMI Oysta’ Fest
summary: They say it was a brave man, or woman, who was the first person to eat an oyster. With that in mind, just wait until you see the sweeping display of raw courage at the Second Annual Block Island Oysta’ Fest, hosted by and on the grounds of the Block Island Maritime Institute on Saturday, August 25.
content:
They say it was a brave man, or woman, who was the first person to eat an oyster. With that in mind, just wait until you see the sweeping display of raw courage at the Second Annual Block Island Oysta’ Fest, hosted by and on the grounds of the Block Island Maritime Institute on Saturday, August 25.

If this year’s event is anything like last year’s, it will be the bivalve bash of the season. Last year, almost 400 people turned out and just about 4000 oysters were consumed from six different oyster farms in Rhode Island. The competition for speediest shucker will be fast and furious, as participants work their knives and their signature techniques around shell after shell, releasing the sweet meat of the sea in the quest for sweet victory, bragging rights, and prizes.

The event runs from 4 p.m. to 6 p.m. and costs $25 per person for lotsa oystas, lotsa hot dogs, chips, wine & beer, music and more. Kids under 8 enter free, as long as they are with a parent. While the oysters will be in the raw, we suggest casual attire for the humans.

meta.title: BIMI Oysta’ Fest
meta.description: The BIMI Oysta’ Fest is on August 25, 2018.

title: Mary D Fundraiser Dinner to Go
summary: The Annual Mary D Fund Dinner-to-Go is on Tuesday Feb 3. Please help us raise money for the Mary D Fund and enjoy a yummy homemade dinner! To order please email Charlotte at cherring@tnc.org or call/text at 401-932-2941 with your dinner selections.
content:
The Annual Mary D Fund Dinner-to-Go is on Tuesday Feb 3. Please help us raise money for the Mary D Fund and enjoy a yummy homemade dinner! To order please email Charlotte at cherring@tnc.org or call/text at 401-932-2941 with your dinner selections.

 

• Pasta with Meatballs

• Pasta with Marinara

• Meatball Grinder

 

All dinners come with salad and dessert. $20 per person. Pick-up at The 1661 Inn between 4:30-6:30

title: Harbor Church Annual Christmas Eve Pageant!
summary: Don't miss our annual Christmas Eve Pageant featuring angels, shepards, wise men, Mary, Joseph and baby Jesus and the telling of the Christmas story!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Annual Roll Call Turkey Dinner at the Harbor Church
summary: You are cordially invited to celebrate a great tradition on the island as Harbor Church serves Its renowned Roll Call dinner.
content:
You are cordially invited to celebrate a great tradition on the island as Harbor Church serves Its renowned Roll Call dinner.
We will be serving a turkey dinner with all the fixin' and dessert from 5-7p.m. this Tuesday night,  October 21st in our main hall.
Take-out drive-through meals will be served in our parking lot from 4:30-5:30.
This is our biggest fund-raiser of the year and we greatly appreciate the support our community has shown over the years.
Please come if you can for this memorable social event.
All meals are by donation.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Clam Shack at Yellow Kittens

author: Austin Crawford
title: Working Waterfronts Resilience Workshop
summary: In our public surveys of where Block Islanders are concerned about sea level rise and coastal resilience, the port of Galilee has been prominently identified.  Galilee provides an essential lifeline for island people, visitors, and businesses.  Let's find out what their concerns are and how we might work together to keep alive the ports that keep us thriving. Please join us for this workshop
content:
In our public surveys of where Block Islanders are concerned about sea level rise and coastal resilience, the port of Galilee has been prominently identified.  Galilee provides an essential lifeline for island people, visitors, and businesses.  Let's find out what their concerns are and how we might work together to keep alive the ports that keep us thriving. Please join us for this workshop 

 

Are you concerned about flooding in yourneighborhood? Interested in tools to helpprepare for coastal storms? Looking forlessons about climate hazards? Join us for aninformative workshop, where we’ll exploreclimate resilience around the Port of Galilee. This event will take place at Narragansett Community Center.

 

Food and a participation stipend included!

 

Click the link to register https://www.eventbrite.com/e/working-waterfronts-resilience-workshop-tickets-1291871485389?aff=oddtdtcreator
